revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mounds}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:kkkk]]" to ""
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mounds}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mounds}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>

The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Fixing the kk issue]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mounds}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: adding defaultsort to all of them
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mounds}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:Completion tier 1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mounds}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Nakazenji Burial Mound Group]] to [[Nakazenji Kofun Group]]: Text replacement - "Burial Mound" to "Kofun"
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mounds}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "X-shaped Kofuns" to "X-shaped Kofun"
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Kofun}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Kofun}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Kofun}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "X-shaped Kofun" to "X-shaped Burial Mound"
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>

The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>

The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />

revision.comment: removing duplicates of this
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市|12=draft}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=draft}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市|12=draft}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=draft}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

revision.comment: adding kofun navbar
revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市|12=draft}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=draft}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市|12=draft}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=draft}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
