Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.text:
'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市|12=draft}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=draft}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional stone coffins were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

'''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi|ja|安来市|12=draft}}, Nishiakaji Town, [[Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=draft}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Tomb Group|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=draft}}. It was designated as a [[Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>

The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=draft}}, and finally to stone coffin chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>

Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-shaped stone coffins at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = 仲仙寺
古墳群| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|所在地。}}}} '''仲仙寺古墳群'''（ちゅうせんじこふんぐん）は、[[島根県]][[安来市]]西赤江町にある[[弥生時代]]後期から[[古墳時代]]後期にかけての[[弥生墳丘墓|墳丘墓群]]および[[古墳群]]。複数の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]を含む。この地域に展開する他の同時期の墳墓群と共に「[[荒島墳墓群]]」を構成する。[[1971年]]（昭和46年）8月12日に国の[[史跡]]に指定され、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）12月23日に追加指定された{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}。 ==地理的・歴史的環境== [[中海]]の南岸に広がる[[安来平野]]の西縁、標高50メートルほどの丘陵の、南北に伸びる尾根筋に沿って墳墓が点在する。史跡仲仙寺古墳群として指定された範囲は、仲仙寺支群と宮山支群に分かれる。前者は1971年に国の[[史跡]]に指定され、後者は1974年に追加指定された{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}。 出雲地方の首長墓は四隅突出型墳丘墓→大型方墳→前方後方墳→石棺式石室と変遷していったことが知られる。本古墳群では仲仙寺支群の8・9・10号墳と宮山支群のIV号墳が四隅突出型墳丘墓であるが、このうち仲仙寺支群の10号墳は消滅している{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}。 == 遺構 == 仲仙寺支群は、もとは四隅突出型墳丘墓3基のほかに方墳8基、円墳7基を含み、計18基以上の墳墓からなっていたが仲仙寺支群の墳墓の数は「しまね観光ナビ」によったが、墳墓数を「19基」とする資料も存在する、大部分は消滅し、現存して史跡に指定されているのは、四隅突出型墳丘墓の8・9号墳のみである。かつては北端に10号墳、その南30メートルに9号墳、その南20メートルに8号墳があり、他の墳墓は東南側の丘陵にあった{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=2,3}}。 8号墳は未発掘のため主体部については未詳である。9号墳の主体部は墳頂部に南・中央・北の3つの土壙があり、それぞれに組合木棺を納めていた。他に墳丘裾部に3基の箱式石棺があった。墳頂部の土壙は中央がもっとも早く造られ、次いで北、南の順に造られたとみられる。中央土壙は二段に造られ、木棺を納めた上を砂で覆っていた。この土壙からは碧玉製管玉が出土した{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4,5}}。 失われた10号墳の主体部は、墳頂部に計11の土壙があり、このうち少なくとも8つは切り合っている（「時期の異なる遺構が部分的に重なり合っている」意）。切り合い状況を分析すると、これら土壙の造られた時期は3期に分けられる。他に墳丘裾部にも組合箱形石棺、石蓋土壙などの埋葬施設があった{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5,6,8,9}}。 宮山支群は、四隅突出型墳丘墓1、前方後方墳2、方墳3、円墳1の計7基からなっていたが、このうち前方後方墳の1号墳は消滅している。 現存各古墳の規模形式等は以下のとおりである{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! 古墳名 !! 形式 !! 規模(m) !! 時代 !! 主体部 |- | 仲仙寺支群8号墳 || 四隅突出型墳丘墓 || 11x11.5以上（突出部除く） || 弥生時代後期 || 未調査 |- | 仲仙寺支群9号墳 || 四隅突出型墳丘墓 || 18.6x16.0（突出部除く） || 弥生時代後期 || 土壙3つ、組合木棺 |- | 宮山支群2号墳 || 1号墳に付属|| || 古墳時代中期 || 未検出 |- | 宮山支群3号墳 || 前方後方墳 || 22 || 弥生時代後期 || 未検出 |- | 宮山支群4号墳 || 方墳 || 12 || 古墳時代中期 || 土壙 |- | 宮山支群IV号墳 || 四隅突出型墳丘墓 || 18.8x15（突出部除く） || 弥生時代後期 ||組合木棺 |- | 宮山支群5号墳 || 円墳 || 径10 - 13 || 古墳時代後期 || 未検出 |- | 宮山支群6号墳 || 方墳 || 7.8 || 古墳時代中期 || 土壙 |} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=仲仙寺古墳群|year=1972|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/13425 全国遺跡報告総覧]（奈良文化財研究所サイト）からダウンロード可。 *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=荒島古墳群・仲仙寺古墳群整備事業報告書|year=2007|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/2639 全国遺跡報告総覧]（奈良文化財研究所サイト）からダウンロード可。 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{coord|35|25|14.5|N|133|12|50.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=title}} {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ちゆうせんしこふんくん}} [[Category:安来市の歴史]] [[category:島根県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:島根県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[仲仙寺古墳群]] to [[Nakazenji Kofun Group]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{Location map|Japan Shimane Prefecture| label = 仲仙寺
古墳群| position = top| mark = Red pog.svg| marksize = 10| relief = 1| width = 250| caption={{left|所在地。}}}} '''仲仙寺古墳群'''（ちゅうせんじこふんぐん）は、[[島根県]][[安来市]]西赤江町にある[[弥生時代]]後期から[[古墳時代]]後期にかけての[[弥生墳丘墓|墳丘墓群]]および[[古墳群]]。複数の[[四隅突出型墳丘墓]]を含む。この地域に展開する他の同時期の墳墓群と共に「[[荒島墳墓群]]」を構成する。[[1971年]]（昭和46年）8月12日に国の[[史跡]]に指定され、[[1974年]]（昭和49年）12月23日に追加指定された{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}。 ==地理的・歴史的環境== [[中海]]の南岸に広がる[[安来平野]]の西縁、標高50メートルほどの丘陵の、南北に伸びる尾根筋に沿って墳墓が点在する。史跡仲仙寺古墳群として指定された範囲は、仲仙寺支群と宮山支群に分かれる。前者は1971年に国の[[史跡]]に指定され、後者は1974年に追加指定された{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}。 出雲地方の首長墓は四隅突出型墳丘墓→大型方墳→前方後方墳→石棺式石室と変遷していったことが知られる。本古墳群では仲仙寺支群の8・9・10号墳と宮山支群のIV号墳が四隅突出型墳丘墓であるが、このうち仲仙寺支群の10号墳は消滅している{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}。 == 遺構 == 仲仙寺支群は、もとは四隅突出型墳丘墓3基のほかに方墳8基、円墳7基を含み、計18基以上の墳墓からなっていたが仲仙寺支群の墳墓の数は「しまね観光ナビ」によったが、墳墓数を「19基」とする資料も存在する、大部分は消滅し、現存して史跡に指定されているのは、四隅突出型墳丘墓の8・9号墳のみである。かつては北端に10号墳、その南30メートルに9号墳、その南20メートルに8号墳があり、他の墳墓は東南側の丘陵にあった{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=2,3}}。 8号墳は未発掘のため主体部については未詳である。9号墳の主体部は墳頂部に南・中央・北の3つの土壙があり、それぞれに組合木棺を納めていた。他に墳丘裾部に3基の箱式石棺があった。墳頂部の土壙は中央がもっとも早く造られ、次いで北、南の順に造られたとみられる。中央土壙は二段に造られ、木棺を納めた上を砂で覆っていた。この土壙からは碧玉製管玉が出土した{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4,5}}。 失われた10号墳の主体部は、墳頂部に計11の土壙があり、このうち少なくとも8つは切り合っている（「時期の異なる遺構が部分的に重なり合っている」意）。切り合い状況を分析すると、これら土壙の造られた時期は3期に分けられる。他に墳丘裾部にも組合箱形石棺、石蓋土壙などの埋葬施設があった{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5,6,8,9}}。 宮山支群は、四隅突出型墳丘墓1、前方後方墳2、方墳3、円墳1の計7基からなっていたが、このうち前方後方墳の1号墳は消滅している。 現存各古墳の規模形式等は以下のとおりである{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}。 {| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! 古墳名 !! 形式 !! 規模(m) !! 時代 !! 主体部 |- | 仲仙寺支群8号墳 || 四隅突出型墳丘墓 || 11x11.5以上（突出部除く） || 弥生時代後期 || 未調査 |- | 仲仙寺支群9号墳 || 四隅突出型墳丘墓 || 18.6x16.0（突出部除く） || 弥生時代後期 || 土壙3つ、組合木棺 |- | 宮山支群2号墳 || 1号墳に付属|| || 古墳時代中期 || 未検出 |- | 宮山支群3号墳 || 前方後方墳 || 22 || 弥生時代後期 || 未検出 |- | 宮山支群4号墳 || 方墳 || 12 || 古墳時代中期 || 土壙 |- | 宮山支群IV号墳 || 四隅突出型墳丘墓 || 18.8x15（突出部除く） || 弥生時代後期 ||組合木棺 |- | 宮山支群5号墳 || 円墳 || 径10 - 13 || 古墳時代後期 || 未検出 |- | 宮山支群6号墳 || 方墳 || 7.8 || 古墳時代中期 || 土壙 |} == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=仲仙寺古墳群|year=1972|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/13425 全国遺跡報告総覧]（奈良文化財研究所サイト）からダウンロード可。 *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=荒島古墳群・仲仙寺古墳群整備事業報告書|year=2007|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/2639 全国遺跡報告総覧]（奈良文化財研究所サイト）からダウンロード可。 == 関連項目 == *[[日本の古墳一覧]] {{coord|35|25|14.5|N|133|12|50.5|E|type:landmark_region:JP-32|display=title}} {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ちゆうせんしこふんくん}} [[Category:安来市の歴史]] [[category:島根県の古墳]] [[Category:日本の墳丘墓]] [[Category:前方後円墳]] [[Category:方墳]] [[Category:島根県にある国指定の史跡]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|qid=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|qid=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|qid=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q103982915]] Wikidata name: Chūsenji Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], Nishiakaji Town, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=仲仙寺古墳群|year=1972|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/13425 Download available from the National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Nara.] *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=荒島古墳群・仲仙寺古墳群整備事業報告書|year=2007|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/2639 Download available from the National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Nara.] == See Also == *[[List of Kofun in Japan]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[d:" to "[[da:d:"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|WD=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982915]] Wikidata name: Chūsenji Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], Nishiakaji Town, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|WD=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982915]] Wikidata name: Chūsenji Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], Nishiakaji Town, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>

The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />

The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>

The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|WD=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982915]] Wikidata name: Chūsenji Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], Nishiakaji Town, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: Bot: add interwikis from Wikidata
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|WD=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982915]] Wikidata name: Chūsenji Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], Nishiakaji Town, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|WD=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982915]] Wikidata name: Chūsenji Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in [[:en:Yasugi, Shimane|Yasugi]], Nishiakaji Town, [[:en:Shimane Prefecture|Shimane Prefecture]]. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|WD=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q103982915]] Wikidata name: Chūsenji Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi}}, Nishiakaji Town, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=仲仙寺古墳群|year=1972|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/13425 Download available from the National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Nara.] *{{Citation|和書|last=安来市教育委員会|title=荒島古墳群・仲仙寺古墳群整備事業報告書|year=2007|publisher=安来市教育委員会}} : [https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/ja/2639 Download available from the National Research Institute for Cultural Properties, Nara.] == See Also == *[[List of Kofun in Japan]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Chūsenji Kofun Cluster]][[ja:仲仙寺古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Yasugi|WD=Q851114}}|coordinates={{Coord|35.42069444444444|N|133.21402777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|part_of={{ill|Q121863310|WD=Q121863310}}|Historic Site={{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}}} '''Chūsenji Kofun Group''' (仲仙寺古墳群) is a collection of {{ill|Yayoi period|en|Yayoi period|WD=Q207858|lt=Yayoi period}} mound tombs and [[kofun]] located in {{ill|Yasugi, Shimane|en|Yasugi, Shimane|WD=Q851114|lt=Yasugi}}, Nishiakaji Town, {{ill|Shimane Prefecture|en|Shimane Prefecture|WD=Q132751|lt=Shimane Prefecture}}. The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

The group includes several {{ill|four-cornered protruding mounds|ja|四隅突出型墳丘墓|1=X-shaped Burial Mound|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/four-cornered protruding mounds|13=User:Immanuelle/X-shaped Burial Mound|qq=}} and forms part of the broader {{ill|Arashima Kofun Cluster|ja|荒島墳墓群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arashima Tomb Group|qq=}}. It was designated as a [[:en:Historic Sites of Japan|national historic site]] on August 12, 1971, with additional designations made on December 23, 1974.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web|和書|title=国指定文化財等データベース|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/401/2220|website=kunishitei.bunka.go.jp|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=文化庁}}</ref>
== Geographic and Historical Context == Located on the western edge of the [[Yasugi Plain]] near the southern shore of [[Lake Nakaumi]], the Chūsenji Kofun Group lies along a ridge approximately 50 meters in elevation. The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !! Burial Chamber |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 8 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 11 x 11.5+ (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Unexcavated |- | Chūsenji Subgroup Tomb 9 || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.6 x 16.0 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || 3 burial pits, wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 3 || Zenpokoho Kofun || 22 || Late Yayoi period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb IV || Four-cornered protruding mound || 18.8 x 15 (excluding protrusions) || Late Yayoi period || Wooden coffins |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 4 || Square mound || 12 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 5 || Round mound || Diameter 10–13 || Late Kofun period || Not detected |- | Miyayama Subgroup Tomb 6 || Square mound || 7.8 || Mid Kofun period || Burial pit |}

The designated site is divided into the Chūsenji and Miyayama subgroups. The former was designated in 1971, and the latter in 1974.<ref name=":A">{{Cite web|和書|title=しまね観光ナビ|url=https://www.kankou-shimane.com/destination/21737|accessdate=2021-02-09|publisher=安来市}}</ref><ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=1}}</ref>
The evolution of burial styles in the Izumo region progressed from four-cornered protruding mounds to large square mounds, then to {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, and finally to Sarcophagus chambers. At this site, four-cornered protruding mounds include Tombs 8, 9, and 10 of the Chūsenji subgroup and Tomb IV of the Miyayama subgroup, although Tomb 10 is no longer extant.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=1}}</ref>
== Structural Features == The Chūsenji subgroup originally comprised at least 18 mounds, including three four-cornered protruding mounds, eight square mounds, and seven round mounds.<ref>Some sources report 19 mounds.</ref> However, most have been lost. Only Tombs 8 and 9, both four-cornered protruding mounds, remain and have been designated as historic sites.<ref name=":A" /> Tomb 8 is unexcavated, and details of its burial chamber are unknown. Tomb 9 features three burial pits—southern, central, and northern—each containing wooden coffins. Additional Sarcophaguss were found at the base of the mound. The central pit was likely the earliest, with the others added later. Artifacts such as jade tubular beads were uncovered from the central pit.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=4}}</ref>
The lost Tomb 10 contained 11 burial pits at its summit, with evidence of overlapping constructions indicating three distinct phases of use. Other features included box-type Sarcophaguss at the base.<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|1972|p=5}}</ref>
The Miyayama subgroup originally had seven mounds, including one four-cornered protruding mound, two {{ill|Zenpokoho Kofun|ja|前方後方墳|lt=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|1=Zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=zenpō-kōhō-fun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoho Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/zenpō-kōhō-fun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, three square mounds, and one round mound. Tomb 1, a Zenpokoho Kofun, is no longer extant.<ref name=":A" />
The details of the remaining tombs are as follows:<ref>{{sfn|安来市教育委員会|2007|p=4}}</ref>
{| class="wikitable" style="background:#ffffff;white-space:nowrap;font-size:95%" ! Tomb Name !! Type !! Dimensions (m) !! Period !!
